result.stdout:
Description:
  Create a backup of all trello boards

Usage:
  trello-backuper.cli [command] [options]

Options:
  --app-key <app-key>  The app key for trello
  --token <token>      The token for trello
  --version            Show version information
  -?, -h, --help       Show help and usage information

Commands:
  backup <targetDirectory>  Create a backup of all trello boards

Description:
  Create a backup of all trello boards

Usage:
  trello-backuper.cli [command] [options]

Options:
  --app-key <app-key>  The app key for trello
  --token <token>      The token for trello
  --version            Show version information

Commands:

result.stdout:
Description:
  Create a backup of all trello boards

Usage:
  trello-backuper.cli backup <targetDirectory> [options]

Arguments:
  <targetDirectory>  The target directory to save the backup to

Options:
  --skip-attachment-download                     Skip the download of attachments
  --skip-json-backup                             Skip saving json files
  --skip-html-creation                           Skip the creation of the html file per board
  --web-hook-url <web-hook-url>                  Call this url every time a back is started, finished or an error occurred. Uses GET-Method if not specified otherwise. See readme for details
  --web-hook-http-method <web-hook-http-method>  Sets the http method used for a web hook. Needs to be combined with --web-hook-url. Allowed methods: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH
  -?, -h, --help                                 Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Create a backup of all trello boards

Usage:
  trello-backuper.cli backup <targetDirectory> [options]

Arguments:
  <targetDirectory>  The target directory to save the backup to

Options:
  --skip-attachment-download                     Skip the download of attachments
  --skip-json-backup                             Skip saving json files
  --skip-html-creation                           Skip the creation of the html file per board
  --web-hook-url <web-hook-url>                  Call this url every time a back is started, finished or an error occurred. Uses GET-Method if not specified otherwise. See readme for details
  --web-hook-http-method <web-hook-http-method>  Sets the http method used for a web hook. Needs to be combined with --web-hook-url. Allowed methods: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH
  -?, -h, --help                                 Show help and usage information
